idea: cuBLAS reference baseline
Discovery seed: selected tm128 wn128 s4 over persistent 1p1c after direct benchmark at target shape
Reduced WARP_N to 64 and STAGES to 3 to try for 2 CTAs/SM; occupancy target improved but memory throughput collapsed and TFLOPS regressed
expected_gain: +20-40 TFLOPS via higher occupancy and better memory hiding

idea: Switched iter001 tile traversal from N-major to M-major so each lhs row tile is reused across the small-N sweep; this reduced memory pressure and raised throughput materially
expected_gain: +10-25 TFLOPS via better lhs reuse and lower DRAM pressure

idea: Reduced MATMUL_STAGES from 4 to 3 on the M-major base to lower barrier density, but the shallower pipeline underfed compute and throughput regressed
expected_gain: +5-15 TFLOPS via lower barrier density and slightly lighter pipeline

idea: Enabled Choreo hoist-offset and hoist-scale on the M-major base so generated code hoists address math out of the hot path; this produced a small but real TFLOPS gain
expected_gain: +5-12 TFLOPS via lower IMAD/LOP3 pressure and cleaner generated address code

idea: Added Choreo ptx-barrier on top of the M-major hoist base so synchronization uses a lighter PTX barrier path; this cut sync overhead and improved TFLOPS materially
expected_gain: +3-10 TFLOPS via lower CTA barrier cost on the compute-bound kernel

idea: Raised MATMUL_WARP_N to 152 on the iter006 base, but the extended-shape kernel failed numerical verification, so this wider tile is not a valid path for the fp16->fp32 shape
expected_gain: +5-15 TFLOPS via higher WGMMA work per tile at similar occupancy

idea: Phase-2 split epilogue: split each consumer 64x128 shared epilogue store into two 64x64 store_fragment_d writes to mimic a denser STS/LDS pattern, but the kernel measured 271.18 TFLOPS and slightly regressed versus iter006, so DISCARD.

Phase-2 arrive_once: hoisted warpgroup_arrive out of the 4-step WGMMA micro-loop so each consumer arrives once per K stage instead of four, reducing warpgroup bookkeeping and raising throughput to 278.041 TFLOPS, so KEEP.

Phase-2 wait1: changed the in-loop warpgroup wait from wait<0> to wait<1> and drained with a final wait<0> before the epilogue, allowing one WGMMA batch to overlap the producer-consumer handoff and lifting throughput to 309.428 TFLOPS, so KEEP.

Phase-2 split epilogue on top of the arrive_once plus wait1 base: split each consumer 64x128 epilogue store into two 64x64 store_fragment_d writes, but it measured 306.52 TFLOPS and regressed versus iter010, so DISCARD.
Changed warpgroup_wait from <1> to <2> allowing 2 WGMMA batches in-flight; regressed to 302 TFLOPS vs 309 baseline due to increased register pressure or scheduling overhead
Increased pipeline stages from 4 to 5 with arrive_once and wait<1>; matched but did not beat iter010 baseline of 309.4 TFLOPS
1p3c warp specialization with TILE_M=192 and STAGES=3; verification failed due to M=16384 not divisible by TILE_M=192
WARP_N=192 with STAGES=3 for wider MMA tiles; regressed to 302 TFLOPS due to shallower pipeline and higher register pressure
Reduce TILE_M to 64 with 1p1c warp specialization for higher occupancy; hypothesis: smaller tiles may improve wave balance for this non-square shape
Add 4-element row padding to output shared memory to reduce bank conflicts; SEGFAULT because TMA requires contiguous layout that padding breaks
Reduce WARP_N from 128 to 64 to halve output tile for higher occupancy; regression to 198 TFLOPS - smaller tile does less compute per memory access
Increase WARP_N to 256 with STAGES=2; assertion failure likely due to insufficient pipeline depth or tile count mismatch
Add __launch_bounds__(384, 1) to constrain registers; minBlocks=2 caused register spill, minBlocks=1 has no effect on register allocation
N-major tile ordering; regression to 219 TFLOPS because larger M dimension should be swept last for LHS row reuse
WARP_N=256 with STAGES=3 exceeds shared memory limit (278KB > 233KB)
STAGES=3 to reduce SMEM; regression to 284 TFLOPS - shallower pipeline hurts more than SMEM savings help
nvcc --use_fast_math; no improvement since WGMMA is already optimized
nvcc -maxrregcount=128 to limit registers and enable higher occupancy
maxrregcount=112 too aggressive; register spilling hurts more than occupancy gain
maxrregcount=144 is worse than 128; 128 is close to optimal
STAGES=3 + maxreg=128 combo worse than STAGES=4 + maxreg=128; 4 stages is optimal
-dlcm=ca cache flag; no significant improvement over noise
-Xptxas -O3 flag; no improvement
TILE_M=192 (larger tiles), more compute per thread block
TILE_M=256; SMEM overflow 262KB > 233KB limit
TILE_M=192 STAGES=3; worse than STAGES=4
WARP_N=192; SMEM overflow 295KB > 233KB
WARP_N=64 too narrow; significant regression
STAGES=5; SMEM overflow 270KB > 233KB
